items:
A **proposition** is a declarative statement that is either 
 true or false.

**Logical connectives** combine propositions: negation ($\lnot$), 
 conjunction ($\land$), disjunction ($\lor$), conditional ($\limplies$), 
 and biconditional ($\liff$).

A **truth table** displays all possible truth values of a 
 compound proposition.

A **tautology** is always true; a **contradiction** is 
 always false.

Two propositions are **logically equivalent** ($\equiv$) if 
 they have identical truth values in all cases.

**De Morgan's Laws**: $\lnot(p \land q) \equiv \lnot p \lor 
 \lnot q$ and $\lnot(p \lor q) \equiv \lnot p \land \lnot q$.

A **predicate** $P(x)$ becomes a proposition when a value is 
 substituted for $x$.

The **universal quantifier** $\forall$ means "for all"; the 
 **existential quantifier** $\exists$ means "there exists."

**Proof techniques**: direct proof, contraposition, 
 contradiction, proof by cases.

content.text: Classify each as proposition, predicate, or neither:

items: Is $\pi$ rational?
Every continuous function is differentiable.
$(p \lor q) \limplies (p \land q)$
$\lnot(p \limplies q) \lor r$
$(p \liff q) \liff (q \liff p)$
$\lnot(p \limplies q)$
$(p \land q) \lor (\lnot p \land q)$
$\lnot((\lnot p) \lor (\lnot q))$

content.text: Negate each statement and express in clear English:

items: Every student submitted homework on time.
There exists a real number whose square is negative.
For every $\epsilon > 0$, there exists $\delta > 0$ such that...

content.text: Prove each statement using an appropriate technique:

items: The sum of two odd integers is even.
If $n^2$ is divisible by 3, then $n$ is divisible by 3.
There is no smallest positive rational number.
